cigna.name: Cigna R49 — E/M Coding Accuracy
cigna.description: Automatic one-level downcode when claim-based diagnosis codes do not appear to justify the E/M complexity level, compared against specialty peer benchmarks.
cigna.bypassRules: Provider must accumulate 5+ adjusted claims, then request bypass. 80% of appealed claims must be upheld to grant bypass.

aetna.name: Aetna CCRP — Claim & Code Review Program
aetna.description: Targets top 3% of in-network providers by coding intensity who submit higher-level E/M codes than CMS/AMA benchmarks. Claims to affect ~3% of providers.
aetna.bypassRules: Practice can pursue early removal by successfully appealing 75% of downcoded claims. Default inclusion duration: 1 year.

united.name: UHC E/M Level Adjustment
united.description: UHC adjusts E/M claims to the level they deem appropriate based on claim data, rather than issuing outright denials. Shifted from denial to downcoding in Q2 2019.
united.bypassRules: No formal bypass program. Individual claim appeals accepted.

bcbs.name: Anthem BCBS E/M Coding Review
bcbs.description: Targets physicians coding at a higher E/M level compared to peers with similar risk-adjusted members. Policy lacks transparency on selection criteria.
bcbs.bypassRules: No formal bypass published. Paused in California pending DMHC review.
